commands.purpose: List role_delegations. --state filters to pending / active / expired / revoked. --role filters to a specific role_id. Ops surface for troubleshooting delegation issues + drafting evidence for compliance queries.

Print full detail for one delegation: identity, role, dates, reason, revocation state, and the audit trail (last N rows from HasActivityLog + HasAudit). Useful when a delegate reports 'why can't I do X'.

Create a delegation via CLI (ops break-glass — the tenant admin surface is the primary path). Goes through the same observer + rules pipeline as the HTTP surface — no fast-path, no observer bypass. --type is one of oof / handover / emergency; emergency requires the delegator to hold the emergency_delegation entitlement.
Revoke a delegation early via CLI. --by defaults to the CLI operator's PlatformUser id (audited as an admin revocation).

Report of delegation usage per tenant + role over a time window. Reads the ApproverResolved-derived usage rows written by RecordDelegationUsageInApproverResolution listener. Answers 'how often did role X's OOF trigger in the last quarter'. Feeds product-team analytics on OOF adoption + friction.
List currently-active impersonation sessions. Ops + security tool for real-time visibility. --tenant filters to a target tenant; --operator filters to a specific PlatformUser.

Print full detail for one session: impersonator (live join OR snapshot fields), impersonated User, target tenant + application, duration, end trigger, audit trail. Also lists every audit row written during the session (via correlation_id join) — the operator's 'blast radius' during the session.

The compliance evidence report. Cross-tenant summary of impersonation activity over the window. Groups by operator + department. Highlights force-terminated sessions + refused attempts. Emits CSV + JSON for downstream SOC 2 evidence submission. This command is what auditors run during evidence review — the same read as the /api/v1/platform/impersonation/audit endpoint, from the CLI.

Emergency CLI-driven force-termination — mirrors POST /impersonation/{session}/force-terminate. Fires ImpersonationSessionForcedTerminate with end_trigger=security_lockdown. Requires --reason (min 40 chars). Used when the HTTP surface is unavailable (rare — SEV-1 scenarios).
